What: Install Vite + React scaffold in frontend/
How: cd frontend && npm create vite@latest . -- --template react && npm install. Verify src/main.jsx and App.jsx exist and npm run dev shows Vite default page on localhost:5173
Notes: Repo already exists — run commands inside existing frontend/ folder

What: Install Tailwind CSS v3 + PostCSS
How: cd frontend && npm install -D tailwindcss@3 postcss autoprefixer && npx tailwindcss init -p. Verify tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js are created.
Notes: Must be v3 NOT v4. Use tailwindcss@3 explicitly

What: Configure tailwind.config.js with brand colors and content paths
How: Open tailwind.config.js. Set content: ['./index.html','./src/**/*.{js,jsx}']. Extend theme with fontFamily inter and colors.brand (green:#16a34a orange:#ea580c) and colors.grade (aplus:#059669 a:#16a34a b:#d97706 c:#c2410c d:#b91c1c f:#7f1d1d)
Notes: Use exact hex values from plan — no changes

What: Set up index.css with Google Fonts + Tailwind directives + design tokens
How: Open src/index.css. Add @import for Inter from Google Fonts at top. Then @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities; Add CSS variables for --color-brand-green --color-brand-orange --color-grade-* under :root. Set body font-family to Inter.
Notes: Import URL: https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700;800

What: Install React Router v6 + axios + lucide-react + recharts
How: cd frontend && npm install react-router-dom axios lucide-react recharts. Verify all appear in package.json dependencies.
Notes: These are the only additional packages — no redux, no zustand, nothing else

What: Create UserContext.jsx with localStorage persistence
How: Create src/context/UserContext.jsx. Export UserProvider component that wraps children in UserContext.Provider with value {currentUser setUser}. useState initializes from JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('reloop_user')). selectUser() updates state AND calls localStorage.setItem. Export useUser hook.
Notes: Dev2 must have GET /api/users working before this is testable

What: Create App.jsx with all 10 routes wired up
How: Create src/App.jsx. Wrap entire tree in <UserProvider>. Use <BrowserRouter><Routes>. Add Route for each: / Marketplace /marketplace/:id /return /return/intercept/:orderId /orders /seller /returns /green-profile /admin. Import all page components even if they are stubs.
Notes: Verify npm run dev shows routes without 404 errors

What: Create stub files for all 10 pages
How:
Create src/pages/Landing.jsx Marketplace.jsx ProductDetail.jsx ReturnFlow.jsx ReturnInterception.jsx OutgrownIt.jsx SellerDashboard.jsx MyReturns.jsx GreenProfile.jsx AdminDashboard.jsx. Each file exports a default function returning

[PageName] — Coming Soon
Notes: Stub pages prevent import errors in App.jsx

What: Create Navbar.jsx component — full implementation
How: Create src/components/Navbar.jsx. Sticky top-0 bg-white border-b border-gray-200 shadow-sm. Left: leaf icon + ReLoop text in brand-green. Center: nav links to /marketplace /orders /returns /green-profile using NavLink with active underline. Right: UserSwitcher component + city badge + green credits chip. Fetch users from VITE_API_URL+/users on mount.
Notes: City badge shows nearestDc city. Credits chip shows currentUser.greenCredits

What: Create UserSwitcher.jsx dropdown component
How: Create src/components/UserSwitcher.jsx. Fetch GET /api/users on mount. Render a styled dropdown <select> showing 'Name • City'. On change parse selected user object and call selectUser() from context. Show currently selected user name as default label.
Notes: Must call selectUser from useUser() context

What: Build Landing page — Hero section + How It Works + Stats
How: In Landing.jsx: Section 1 hero — H1 'Every returned product deserves a second life' + subtext + two CTA buttons (Browse Marketplace and Return an Item). Section 2 — 4-step How It Works with icons. Section 3 — fetch GET /api/stats and display 4 stat cards (products saved CO2 items sold credits issued). Section 4 — 3 persona cards. Use Tailwind only.
Notes: Stats section can show 0 if API not ready — add loading skeleton

What: Create StatCard.jsx reusable component
How: Create src/components/StatCard.jsx. Props: icon value label unit color. Renders a white rounded-xl shadow-sm p-6 card with the icon on top value in large bold font label below. Used on Landing and Admin pages.
Notes: Accept any Lucide icon as prop

What: Init Express backend and install all dependencies
How: cd backend && npm init -y && npm install express cors dotenv multer @aws-sdk/client-s3 uuid @google/generative-ai @prisma/client && npm install -D nodemon prisma. Add scripts to package.json: dev: nodemon src/index.js and start: node src/index.js
Notes: Do NOT install TypeScript or ts-node — pure JavaScript

What: Create backend/src/index.js — Express app entry
How: Create src/index.js. Import express cors dotenv. Call dotenv.config(). Init app. Add cors({origin:'*'}) middleware. Add express.json({limit:'50mb'}) and express.urlencoded. Mount all route stubs. Add GET /api/health returning {status:'ok'}. Listen on PORT from env or 5000.
Notes: Test with curl http://localhost:5000/api/health — should return {status:ok}

What: Create all route stub files in src/routes/
How: Create files: users.js marketplace.js orders.js returns.js grading.js seller.js batchGrade.js greenCredits.js admin.js stats.js outgrown.js. Each file: const router = require('express').Router(); router.get('/' (req res) => res.json({message:'TODO'})); module.exports = router;
Notes: Mount all in index.js with app.use('/api/routename' require('./routes/file'))

What: Implement GET /api/users endpoint
How: In src/routes/users.js query prisma.user.findMany() with include:{nearestDc:true}. Return array of users with fields: id name email city state greenCredits greenTier and nearestDc object. Handle errors with try/catch and 500 response.
Notes: Dev1 Navbar depends on this working by end of Day 1

What: Implement GET /api/stats endpoint
How: In src/routes/stats.js query DB for: count of returns by status count of inventory items by status total greenCredits awarded. Calculate CO2 saved as (count of LISTED+SOLD+DONATED items * 0.45kg). Return object with totalReturns totalListed totalSold totalDonated totalRecycled co2SavedKg totalGreenCredits.
Notes: CO2 formula: each resold item saves ~0.45kg vs new product manufacturing

What: Create src/lib/db.js Prisma client singleton
How: Create src/lib/db.js. const { PrismaClient } = require('@prisma/client'); const prisma = new PrismaClient(); module.exports = { prisma }; Import this in every route file.
Notes: Never import PrismaClient directly in route files — always use this singleton

What: Create backend/.env file with all variables
How: Create backend/.env with: DATABASE_URL GEMINI_API_KEY AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY AWS_REGION S3_BUCKET_NAME PORT=5000. Use placeholder values for now — Dev3 will fill real RDS URL.
Notes: Never commit .env to git — add to .gitignore immediately

What: Add .gitignore files for both frontend and backend
How: Create backend/.gitignore with: node_modules/ .env dist/ .prisma/. Create frontend/.gitignore with: node_modules/ .env dist/ .vite/
Notes: Critical — env files must NOT be pushed to GitHub

What: Init Prisma in backend directory
How: cd backend && npx prisma init. This creates prisma/ folder with schema.prisma and adds DATABASE_URL to .env. Verify prisma/schema.prisma file exists.
Notes: Must run from inside backend/ directory not root

What: Write complete Prisma schema.prisma
How: Open prisma/schema.prisma. Write all 10 models: Product User DeliveryCenter DcRoute Order Return ReturnImage AiGrading InventoryItem MarketplaceOrder GradeConfig GreenCreditLedger. Copy exact schema from implementation_plan.md — all @map decorators @db.Decimal types and @@index directives must be included.
Notes: Double check all relations are bidirectional — mismatched relations cause migration errors

What: Run Prisma migration to create database tables
How: npx prisma migrate dev --name init. This generates SQL and applies it to DATABASE_URL. Verify all 12 tables are created with npx prisma studio — opens browser UI at localhost:5555 showing all tables.
Notes: Requires DATABASE_URL to be set. Use local postgres for day 1 — switch to RDS URL on Day 5

What: Create backend/src/lib/constants.js with all cost constants
How: Create src/lib/constants.js with COSTS object (LAST_MILE_PICKUP:65 LAST_MILE_DELIVERY:65 HANDLING_TOTAL:42 MIN_MARGIN:20 etc) GRADE_CONFIG object (A+ through F with label discountPct route minMrp) and GREEN_CREDITS object. Export all three with module.exports.
Notes: These values are used in routing and costing logic — must match business rules exactly

What: Start writing prisma/seed.js — Delivery Centers and DC Routes
How: Create prisma/seed.js. Import PrismaClient. Write createMany for 6 DCs: Delhi(DEL1) Mumbai(MUM1) Bangalore(BLR1) Kolkata(KOL1) Hyderabad(HYD1) Jaipur(JAI1). Then write 36 DC routes (6x6 matrix) with distance transferCost estimatedDays. Use upsert or deleteMany first to allow re-seeding.
Notes: Jaipur DC is critical for Priya's demo scenario — nearestDc for user-009

What: Seed products — all 27 items (20 original + 7 cheap)
How: In seed.js add createMany for 27 products. Original 20 from demo_blueprint.md (Samsung Nike Sony etc). Plus 7 cheap ones from persona_fixes.md: prod-021 Bata shoes Rs499 prod-022 Spigen case Rs299 prod-023 Handkerchiefs Rs199 prod-024 Pop Socket Rs99 prod-025 Mi Camera Rs2499 prod-026 Kurti Rs599 prod-027 Dupatta Rs349.
Notes: Image URLs use /images/filename.jpg — Dev1 downloads placeholder images to frontend/public/images/

What: Seed all 14 users across 6 cities
How: In seed.js add createMany for 14 users: 8 original (user-001 through user-008) plus Priya Verma in Jaipur (user-009) Kavita Singh Deepa Rathore in Jaipur (user-010 user-011) Rahul Deshmukh in Pune (user-012) Neha Patil Aarti Joshi in Pune (user-013 user-014). Set nearestDcId correctly for each.
Notes: Priya (user-009) nearestDcId must be dc-jai-001 (Jaipur DC) — this is critical for routing demo

What: Seed all 19 orders
How: In seed.js add createMany for 19 orders. Orders ord-001 through ord-015 from demo_blueprint.md plus ord-016 (Priya's Rs499 shoes) ord-017 (Spigen case) ord-018 (Pop Socket) ord-019 (Rahul baby monitor). Set status correctly: RETURN_REQUESTED for most DELIVERED for Rahul's monitor and Priya's shoes.
Notes: ord-016 (Priya shoes) must have status DELIVERED so interception flow can trigger

What: Build Marketplace page — header + filter bar + product grid layout
How: In Marketplace.jsx: Header section showing current user name and city (from useUser context). Filter bar with category dropdown sort dropdown and grade checkboxes using Tailwind select and input classes. Grid layout using grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6. Fetch GET /api/marketplace?user_id=X on mount and re-fetch when user changes.
Notes: Show skeleton loading cards (animate-pulse gray boxes) while fetching

What: Build ProductCard.jsx component — full design
How: Create src/components/ProductCard.jsx. Props: item (inventory item with product and pricing). White bg rounded-xl shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition. Image top (h-48 object-cover). GradeBadge component below image. Product name in font-semibold. MRP with line-through text-gray-400. ReLoop price in large text-brand-green font-bold. Shipping cost in small text. Near You badge in green pill if isNearYou. Bottom: discount % badge + green credits chip + View Details button.
Notes: isNearYou prop comes from API — shows 'Near you! Fast delivery' badge

What: Build GradeBadge.jsx component
How: Create src/components/GradeBadge.jsx. Props: grade. Returns a <span> pill with text and background color based on grade. A+=bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-800. A=bg-green-100 text-green-800. B=bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800. C=bg-orange-100 text-orange-800. D=bg-red-100 text-red-800. F=bg-red-200 text-red-900.
Notes: Used in ProductCard HealthCard and Seller Dashboard

What: Build ProductDetail page — image gallery + price section
How: In ProductDetail.jsx: Fetch GET /api/marketplace/:id?user_id=X on mount. Left column: main image + thumbnail strip. Right column: product name grade brand condition label. Price section: MRP crossed out → ReLoop price → shipping breakdown. CostBreakdown component showing transfer + last mile separately. HealthCard component below. Buy Now button.
Notes: Use useParams() from react-router-dom to get :id

What: Build HealthCard.jsx component
How: Create src/components/HealthCard.jsx. Props: grading. White rounded-xl border border-gray-200 p-6. Header 'Product Health Card' with shield icon. Grade score as a circular progress or just a large number out of 100. Confidence % in small text. conditionSummary paragraph. Defects list — if defectsFound is empty show green checkmark 'No defects found'. Missing parts section if any. functionalNotes section.
Notes: All data comes from the grading object returned by the API

What: Build CostBreakdown.jsx component
How: Create src/components/CostBreakdown.jsx. Props: transferCost lastMile totalShipping sellingPrice mrp. Renders a breakdown table: Original MRP (crossed) → ReLoop Price → DC Transfer cost → Last Mile cost → Total You Pay. Each row left-aligned label right-aligned amount. Total row in bold border-t.
Notes: Used in ProductDetail and also shown in Return Flow

What: Add user switching to Marketplace — re-fetch on user change
How: In Marketplace.jsx: use useEffect with [currentUser] as dependency so products re-fetch when user changes in Navbar. Show a subtle toast or banner 'Showing prices for [City]' when user switches. Shipping costs should visibly change for items coming from distant DCs.
Notes: This is a key demo moment — switching from Mumbai to Kolkata changes shipping shown on cards

What: Download + place placeholder product images in frontend/public/images/
How: Download or generate placeholder product images for all 27 products. Name them exactly as imageUrl field in seed data (bata_shoes.jpg samsung_m34.jpg etc). Place in frontend/public/images/. If no real image use a colored placeholder with product name using a service like via.placeholder.com or generate with AI.
Notes: Image filenames must match exactly what is in the seed data imageUrl field

What: Implement GET /api/marketplace?user_id= endpoint
How: In src/routes/marketplace.js: fetch all InventoryItems where status=AVAILABLE. Include product currentDc. Fetch buyer's nearestDcId. Fetch all dc_routes. For each item call calculateDeliveryCost(item.currentDcId buyer.nearestDcId routes). Call calculateViability(product.mrp grading.gradeDiscountPct totalShipping). Filter out items where viable=false. Add isNearYou flag. Return sorted array.
Notes: This is the most complex API — test with multiple user_id values to verify different pricing

What: Implement GET /api/marketplace/:id?user_id= endpoint
How: In src/routes/marketplace.js add router.get('/:id'). Fetch single InventoryItem with includes: product currentDc grading return. Fetch buyer user and routes. Calculate per-buyer cost breakdown. Return item with full grading health card data plus per-buyer pricing breakdown object.
Notes: Must include grading object — HealthCard component depends on it

What: Implement GET /api/orders?user_id= endpoint
How: In src/routes/orders.js: fetch all Orders where userId=user_id. Include product. Add outgrownEligible flag: true if status=DELIVERED and no Return record exists for that order. Return sorted by orderedAt desc.
Notes: outgrownEligible flag powers the Outgrown It page — critical for Rahul demo

What: Write src/lib/costing.js with calculateDeliveryCost and calculateViability
How: Create src/lib/costing.js. calculateDeliveryCost(sourceDcId destDcId dcRoutes): find route in array return {transferCost lastMile totalShipping estimatedDays}. Default to transferCost=50 if no route found. calculateViability(mrp gradeDiscountPct shippingCost): compute sellingPrice as mrp*(1-discountPct/100) totalCost as HANDLING_TOTAL+shippingCost+MIN_MARGIN profit=sellingPrice-totalCost return {viable sellingPrice totalCost profit}.
Notes: Import COSTS from constants.js — do not hardcode values

What: Test Marketplace API with Postman or curl for 3 different users
How: Run: curl 'http://localhost:5000/api/marketplace?user_id=user-003' (Vikram Mumbai). Then user-006 (Sneha Kolkata). Then user-009 (Priya Jaipur). Verify: different shipping costs appear. Rs.99 pop socket NOT returned for anyone (non-viable). Allen Solly isNearYou=true for Vikram (both at MUM1).
Notes: Screenshot the 3 different responses as evidence for demo

What: Complete seed.js — all 18 returns with correct statuses
How: In seed.js add createMany for 18 returns. ret-001 through ret-015 from demo_blueprint.md at various statuses (LISTED ROUTED RECEIVED_AT_DC SOLD). Add ret-016 (Priya shoes) status=INITIATED currentDcId=null (not picked up yet — interception triggers here). ret-017 (Spigen case) status=LISTED. ret-018 (Pop Socket) status=ROUTED route=DONATE.
Notes: ret-013 and ret-014 must be RECEIVED_AT_DC with NO grading record — these are the live demo items

What: Complete seed.js — all 13 AI gradings
How: In seed.js add creates for 13 AiGrading records. grade-001 through grade-012 from demo_blueprint.md. Add grade-017 for Spigen case (A+ 99 RESELL). Add grade-018 for Pop Socket (A DONATE — route DONATE because Rs79 < Rs147 cost). Do NOT add grading for ret-013 (boAt earbuds) or ret-014 (Atomic Habits) — these are graded live in demo.
Notes: grade_discount_pct must match GRADE_CONFIG values — A=20 A+=10 B=35

What: Complete seed.js — inventory items + marketplace order + green credits
How: Add 8 InventoryItems (inv-001 through inv-007 plus inv-008 for Spigen case). Add marketplace_order mp-ord-001 (Karthik bought Sony headphones). Add GradeConfig table entries for A+ A B C D F. Add green_credits_ledger entry for Karthik. Set prisma seed script in package.json and run npx prisma db seed.
Notes: Verify seed completes without errors. Run npx prisma studio to visually confirm all data

What: Write src/lib/routing.js with decideRoute function
How: Create src/lib/routing.js. Function decideRoute(grade mrp sellingPrice totalCost hasLocalBuyers): Evaluate RESELL viability (sellingPrice > totalCost AND grade in A/A+/B AND mrp >= minMrp). Evaluate REFURBISH if grade B or C. Evaluate DONATE if not viable and grade not F. Evaluate RECYCLE if grade F or safety hazard. Return {chosenRoute reason alternatives:[{route viable reason sellingPrice totalCost profit}]} for all 4 routes.
Notes: alternatives array powers the route comparison shown in Return Flow page

What: Test seed data in Prisma Studio
How: Run npx prisma studio. Navigate to each table. Verify: 27 products 14 users 6 DCs 36 routes 19 orders 18 returns 13 gradings 8 inventory items. Check Priya user-009 has nearestDcId=dc-jai-001. Check ret-013 has no grading record. Check Pop Socket grade-018 has routeDecision=DONATE.
Notes: Take a screenshot of Prisma Studio as reference during demo

What: Build ReturnFlow page — 6-step wizard with StepIndicator
How: In ReturnFlow.jsx: Use useState for currentStep (1-6). Create StepIndicator component showing step numbers with active/completed styling. Step 1: fetch user orders filtered to DELIVERED status — show list with product name image price — user clicks one to select. Step 2: Return reason radio buttons (SIZE_FIT CHANGED_MIND QUALITY NOT_AS_DESCRIBED DEFECTIVE). Step 3: Auto-redirect to /return/intercept/:orderId if item is interception-eligible.
Notes: Pass selected order and reason as URL params or state to ReturnInterception page

What: Build ReturnFlow Step 4 — Image upload section
How: Step 4 in ReturnFlow: Show ImageUpload component. Instructions: 'Upload 2-5 clear photos of the product'. Below upload area show preview thumbnails with X buttons to remove. Next button disabled until at least 2 images uploaded. Store images as File objects in component state.
Notes: Images are sent to POST /api/grading as multipart form data

What: Build ImageUpload.jsx drag-and-drop component
How: Create src/components/ImageUpload.jsx. Drag-and-drop zone with dashed border — ondragover highlight with blue border. Also show Browse Files button using hidden file input. Accept image/* only. Max 5 files 5MB each — show error if exceeded. On file add call onFilesChange prop callback. Preview thumbnails in a horizontal strip with remove buttons.
Notes: Use URL.createObjectURL() for preview thumbnails without uploading yet

What: Build ReturnFlow Step 5 — AI grading live animation + result display
How: Step 5: On Next from Step 4 immediately call POST /api/grading with FormData. While waiting show animated spinner with text 'AI analyzing your product... This takes about 2 seconds'. On response reveal grade with a card animation. Show: grade letter score/100 confidence % conditionSummary defects found. Use CSS animation for the grade reveal (scale from 0 to 1).
Notes: This is the WOW moment of the demo — animation must be smooth and impressive

What: Build ReturnFlow Step 6 — Route decision with alternatives
How: Step 6: After grading show route decision. Main recommendation card (RESELL/REFURBISH/DONATE/RECYCLE) with icon and explanation. Below show 4 alternative route cards each showing: route name viable/not-viable sellingPrice totalCost profit. Non-viable routes shown in gray with X icon and reason ('Selling price Rs79 < minimum cost Rs147'). Add RouteVisualization component showing DC journey on map.
Notes: alternatives array comes from the routing API response

What: Build RouteVisualization.jsx component
How: Create src/components/RouteVisualization.jsx. Props: sourceDcCity destDcCity transferCost lastMile totalShipping. Shows a horizontal flow: [Source DC City] --transfer Rs.X--> [Dest DC City] --last mile Rs.65--> [Customer]. Use Tailwind flex and border-dotted styling. Show distance and estimated days below each arrow.
Notes: Keep it simple — no actual map library needed. Text-based flow diagram is fine

What: Build ReturnInterception page — 3-option cards
How: In ReturnInterception.jsx: Fetch order details from URL param. Show product name and image at top. Show 3 option cards stacked: STANDARD RETURN (Rs.499 7-10 days no credits gray border). EXPRESS RETURN + DONATE (Rs.499 instant + 100 credits recommended green border). RETURN & RESELL ON RELOOP (Rs.499 instant + 50 credits + demand signal blue border). On Select POST /api/returns/:id/method then redirect to /returns.
Notes: recommendedOption: DONATE if item is cheap (mrp < Rs.500) RESELL if multiple local buyers found. Show 'X buyers near [City] want this!' only for RESELL option

What: Build StepIndicator.jsx component
How: Create src/components/StepIndicator.jsx. Props: totalSteps currentStep labels. Renders horizontal row of numbered circles connected by lines. Completed steps show check icon in green. Current step shows number in filled blue. Future steps show number in gray outline. Labels below each circle in small text.
Notes: Used only in ReturnFlow page

What: Implement POST /api/returns endpoint
How: In src/routes/returns.js: Validate orderId userId reason in body. Check if order exists and belongs to user. Create Return record with status=INITIATED. Query DB to check interception eligibility: count inventory items at user's nearestDc in same category AND selling price < product MRP*0.8. Return {returnId interceptionEligible localBuyerCount suggestedRoute}.
Notes: interceptEligible=true triggers redirect to ReturnInterception page in frontend

What: Implement POST /api/returns/:id/method endpoint
How: In returns.js: Validate returnId and method (STANDARD|EXPRESS_DONATE|RELOOP_RESELL). Update Return record: set returnMethod. If EXPRESS_DONATE or RELOOP_RESELL set status=INITIATED and refund_amount stays same. Award green credits: RELOOP_RESELL=50 EXPRESS_DONATE=100 STANDARD=0. Insert into green_credits_ledger. Return updated return record.
Notes: Green credits award must also update users.greenCredits field

What: Implement POST /api/grading endpoint with multer + file handling
How: In src/routes/grading.js: Use multer({storage:memoryStorage()}) middleware. Accept up to 5 files in 'images' field. For each file convert buffer to base64. Call gradeProduct() from lib/gemini.js. Upload each image to S3 using uploadToS3(). Save AiGrading to DB. Call decideRoute() from lib/routing.js. Update Return status to GRADED. Return {grading routing}.
Notes: multer memoryStorage keeps files in RAM — not disk — so we can pass buffer directly to S3

What: Implement GET /api/returns?user_id= endpoint
How: In returns.js: fetch all Returns where userId=user_id. Include order product currentDc grading. Sort by createdAt desc. Return array. Also add GET /api/returns/:id that returns single return with all includes for the My Returns detail view.
Notes: My Returns page uses this to show status timeline

What: Test full return + grading flow end to end
How: Use Postman: POST /api/returns with valid orderId userId reason. Note returnId. Then POST /api/grading with returnId + 2 real product photos as multipart. Verify Gemini response is valid JSON with grade score etc. Verify S3 image URLs are returned. Verify AiGrading record saved in DB.
Notes: If Gemini quota hit (15 RPM) wait 4 seconds between requests during testing

What: Implement src/lib/gemini.js — full Gemini API wrapper
How: Create src/lib/gemini.js. Import GoogleGenerativeAI from @google/generative-ai. Init with GEMINI_API_KEY env var. Export async gradeProduct(images category returnReason productName mrp). Build prompt from implementation_plan.md exactly. Map images array to inlineData parts. Call model.generateContent. Strip markdown code fences from response. JSON.parse and return.
Notes: Test with a real product image. If parse fails add error logging of raw text before throwing

What: Implement src/lib/s3.js — AWS S3 upload helper
How: Create src/lib/s3.js. Import S3Client PutObjectCommand from @aws-sdk/client-s3. Import uuid v4. Init S3Client with region and credentials from env. Export async uploadToS3(buffer mimeType folder). Generate unique key as folder/uuid.ext. Send PutObjectCommand. Return public URL as https://bucket.s3.region.amazonaws.com/key.
Notes: S3 bucket must have public read enabled or use signed URLs. For hackathon public bucket is fine

What: Test Gemini + S3 integration with a standalone test script
How: Create a test file backend/test-gemini.js. Import gradeProduct from lib/gemini.js. Load a local image file as base64. Call gradeProduct with test data. Console.log the result. Run with node test-gemini.js. Verify grade score and conditionSummary fields are present.
Notes: Delete test file before demo — do not commit API keys

What: Add demand signal helper function
How: In src/lib/routing.js add async getDemandSignal(productId dcId prisma). Query prisma.user.count where nearestDcId=dcId AND orders.some.product.category = product.category. Return count as integer. This powers the 'X buyers near you want this' display in Return Interception and Outgrown It.
Notes: This is a proxy for real demand data — good enough for hackathon

What: Build SellerDashboard page — batch upload zone + processing animation
How: In SellerDashboard.jsx: Top section with batch upload drop zone (large dashed box with camera icon 'Drag return photos here — up to 200 files'). On files dropped show file count badge and Enable button 'Grade X Items'. On click start animated counter: show progress bar counting from 0 to 100% with timer showing elapsed seconds. After API response show results.
Notes: Simulate 42-second feel: show progress animation for 2-3 seconds regardless of actual API speed

What: Build SellerDashboard results table + summary stats
How: After batch grading response: Show summary cards — Total Graded: 200 Resellable: 170 Recovery: Rs.2.13L Time Saved: 10 hrs. Below show results table with columns: Item photo Grade Suggested Price Route Action. Color code rows by grade. Show [Auto-List All A/A+] and [Process All Routes] buttons at bottom.
Notes: Table rows should be expandable — click row to see AI assessment details

What: Build expandable row in Seller table — AI detail view
How: Each table row in Seller results: click to expand. Expanded section shows: 3 thumbnails of uploaded photos. AI Assessment block: score confidence defectsFound list. Suggested price (editable input pre-filled with AI price). Route badge. Three action buttons: Accept Override Flag for Review.
Notes: Override button allows seller to change grade manually — POST /api/seller/returns/:id/override

What: Build SellerDashboard analytics section — return reason insights
How: Below results table add Seller Analytics section. Show bar chart using Recharts BarChart: return reasons breakdown (SIZE_FIT 68% CHANGED_MIND 20% QUALITY 12%). Show insight card: 'Tip: 45% of your Kurti returns are size issues. Update your size chart to reduce returns.' Use Recharts PieChart for grade distribution.
Notes: Data comes from GET /api/seller/returns?seller_id=X analytics field in response

What: Build OutgrownIt page — past orders with Outgrown It button
How: In OutgrownIt.jsx: Fetch GET /api/orders?user_id=X and filter to outgrownEligible=true. Show each order as a card with product image name purchase date original price. On each card show 'Outgrown It?' button in green. On click: fetch demand signal and AI price estimate. Expand card to show: demand count 'X buyers in [City] want this' AI price range Rs.X-Rs.Y List in 1 Click button.
Notes: Switch to Rahul (user-012) to see baby monitor with demand signal 47 parents

What: Build OutgrownIt — 1-click listing modal
How: When List in 1 Click is clicked: show a simple modal. Show product name original image. Show condition selector: Like New / Good / Used. Show estimated price (editable). Show 'Confirm Listing' button. On confirm POST /api/outgrown. On success show green success card: 'Listed! Buyers in [City] will be notified.'
Notes: Modal should be minimal — condition + confirm. No re-photography needed (original Amazon images reused)

What: Build AdminDashboard page — full system overview
How: In AdminDashboard.jsx: Fetch GET /api/admin/overview. Show top row of stat cards: Total Returns Today/Total Graded/On Marketplace/Sold/Donated/Recycled. Show Recharts PieChart for route distribution (Resell/Refurbish/Donate/Recycle). Show Recharts BarChart for grade distribution (A+ through F). Show returns table with all returns sortable by status date grade.
Notes: Admin page is for judges to see the full intelligence at a glance

What: Build DC Overview section in Admin page
How: Below charts in AdminDashboard: show a 6-row table for each DC showing: DC name city type inventory count (AVAILABLE/SOLD). A section titled 'Cost Savings' showing: Total logistics cost old system Rs.X vs ReLoop Rs.Y savings Rs.Z. Use calculated values from stats API.
Notes: Cost savings calculation: count items * Rs440 (old) vs count * Rs147 (ReLoop avg) = savings

What: Implement POST /api/seller/batch-grade endpoint
How: In src/routes/batchGrade.js: Accept multipart with up to 200 files and a sellerId field. Group files by product (by filename prefix or order). For each group call gradeProduct() from gemini.js. Rate limit to max 15 RPM (Gemini free tier) by adding 4 second delays between calls. Return array of {filename grade score route suggestedPrice} for all items.
Notes: 15 RPM limit means 200 items takes 200/15 * 60 = 800 seconds. For demo use max 5 images. In demo we SHOW 200 results from seed data — not actually process 200.

What: Implement GET /api/seller/returns?seller_id= with analytics
How: In src/routes/seller.js: Fetch returns where order.product belongs to a seller (for hackathon use a fake seller_id check or just fetch all returns for user-003 as seller). Include grading. Compute analytics: returnReasonsBreakdown as count by reason. gradeDistribution as count by grade. recoveryRate as (resellable / total). Return returns array plus analytics object.
Notes: Seller persona maps to user-003 Vikram for demo — hardcode this for hackathon simplicity

What: Implement POST /api/outgrown endpoint
How: In src/routes/outgrown.js: Accept {orderId userId conditionLabel}. Verify order is DELIVERED and no return exists. Fetch product. Create InventoryItem with source=OUTGROWN grade=A (assumed for outgrown listings) basePrice=product.mrp*0.6. Return created inventory item plus demand signal count.
Notes: source=OUTGROWN distinguishes from returned items in marketplace filtering

What: Implement GET /api/admin/overview endpoint
How: In src/routes/admin.js: Query returns counts by status. Query inventory counts by status. Query marketplace orders count. Query total green credits. Compute per-DC inventory counts. Return comprehensive object with: returnsByStatus inventoryByStatus inventoryByGrade dcInventory totalGreenCredits totalCO2Saved costSavingsVsOldSystem.
Notes: costSavingsVsOldSystem = count(items) * (440 - average_reloop_cost)

What: Implement POST /api/marketplace/:id/buy endpoint
How: In src/routes/marketplace.js add router.post('/:id/buy'). Accept {buyerId}. Verify inventory item is AVAILABLE. Calculate shipping cost. Create MarketplaceOrder. Update InventoryItem status to SOLD. Update Order status to SOLD (linked return). Award green credits to buyer (INSERT into green_credits_ledger UPDATE users.greenCredits). Return marketplace order.
Notes: This completes the full product lifecycle visible in Admin dashboard

What: Implement GET /api/green-credits?user_id= endpoint
How: In src/routes/greenCredits.js: Fetch user with greenCredits greenTier. Fetch green_credits_ledger where userId sorted by createdAt desc. Compute tier based on credits: 0-99=SEEDLING 100-499=SAPLING 500-1999=TREE 2000+=FOREST. Return {balance tier history nextTier nextTierAt}.
Notes: Tier thresholds must match what Green Profile page displays

What: Create backend/src/lib/db.js Prisma singleton (if not done Day 1)
How: Verify src/lib/db.js exists and exports {prisma}. If not create it now. Import in every route file that needs DB access. Run a quick test query to confirm connection works.
Notes: All route files should use const {prisma} = require('../lib/db') at top

What: Add GradeConfig seed data and verify routing uses it
How: In seed.js add GradeConfig records: A+(10% RESELL minMrp=100) A(20% RESELL 150) B(35% RESELL 3000) C(50% REFURBISH null) D(0% DONATE null) F(0% RECYCLE null). Re-run npx prisma db seed. In routing.js use GradeConfig.minMrp when checking if RESELL is viable.
Notes: GradeConfig in DB means business rules can be changed without code changes

What: Verify the Rs99 Pop Socket smart donate scenario works end-to-end
How: Using Prisma Studio or curl: Confirm ret-018 (Pop Socket) has grading with routeDecision=DONATE. Confirm grade-018 has estimatedResaleValue=0. Call GET /api/marketplace?user_id=user-001 and verify Pop Socket does NOT appear in results (non-viable). This is a key demo moment.
Notes: If it appears in results there's a bug in calculateViability — selling price Rs79 must be < totalCost Rs127 (local) so viable=false

What: Load test the API with multiple concurrent requests
How: Use curl or simple Node script to call GET /api/marketplace with 5 different user IDs simultaneously. Verify all return correct different pricing. Verify no Prisma connection pool errors. If needed add connection pooling or increase pool size in PrismaClient config.
Notes: During demo we switch users rapidly — must not timeout or crash

What: Build MyReturns page — timeline view
How: In MyReturns.jsx: Fetch GET /api/returns?user_id=X. For each return show a card with product image name return reason and a status timeline. Timeline: INITIATED -> PICKED_UP -> RECEIVED_AT_DC -> GRADED -> ROUTED -> LISTED/DONATED/RECYCLED. Current status highlighted in green. Past steps filled. Future steps gray. Show date timestamps on completed steps.
Notes: ReturnFlow submits returns — MyReturns shows the result

What: Build GreenProfile page — credits + tier + impact
How: In GreenProfile.jsx: Fetch GET /api/green-credits?user_id=X. Show at top: large credit balance number + tier badge (Seedling Sapling Tree Forest) with leaf icon. Progress bar to next tier. Below: impact stats 'You gave X products a second life' 'Saved X kg CO2'. Credits history table: date action amount description.
Notes: Tier colors: Seedling=green Sapling=emerald Tree=teal Forest=cyan

What: Add skeleton loaders to all pages that fetch data
How: In Marketplace ProductDetail ReturnFlow AdminDashboard SellerDashboard: Add isLoading state. While isLoading=true show skeleton cards using animate-pulse bg-gray-200 rounded placeholders. On data load switch to real content. This makes the app feel polished and handles slow network.
Notes: Skeleton cards should match the approximate shape of real cards

What: Add error and empty states to all list pages
How: For Marketplace: if no items returned show 'No products available in your area right now'. For MyReturns: if no returns show 'You have no returns yet — try returning a product'. For OutgrownIt: if no eligible orders show 'No past orders eligible for resale'. All empty states have an icon and a call-to-action link.
Notes: Empty states prevent jarring blank white pages during demo

What: Mobile responsiveness pass on all pages
How: Go through each page and add Tailwind responsive prefixes: sm: md: lg:. Navbar: hamburger menu on mobile. Product grid: 1 col on mobile 2 on sm 3 on lg. ProductDetail: stack columns vertically on mobile. Seller table: horizontal scroll on small screens.
Notes: Judges may view on laptop — ensure 1280px width looks perfect. Mobile is bonus

What: Final UI polish — hover transitions animations review
How: Add hover:shadow-lg transition-shadow to all cards. Add hover:bg-gray-50 to table rows. Ensure all buttons have hover states. Add transition-all duration-200 to interactive elements. Review spacing — ensure consistent padding across all pages using Tailwind p-4 p-6 p-8.
Notes: Takes 2-3 hours — do this LAST as it requires visual review

What: Create frontend/.env with VITE_API_URL pointing to EC2
How: Create or update frontend/.env: VITE_API_URL=http://YOUR_EC2_IP:5000/api. In src/lib/api.js set baseURL as import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL. Rebuild frontend: npm run build. Test that it hits EC2 not localhost.
Notes: Switch from localhost to EC2 URL on Day 5 after backend is deployed

What: Final demo walkthrough — test all 7 demo scenarios
How: Follow demo script exactly: Landing → Return Interception (switch to Priya) → Marketplace (switch users show different prices) → Product Detail (health card) → Return Flow (grade boAt earbuds live) → Seller Dashboard (batch grade demo) → Outgrown It (Rahul baby monitor). Fix any broken links or missing data.
Notes: Allocate 2 hours for this. Fix issues immediately. Do not skip any demo step

What: Implement POST /api/green-credits/award endpoint
How: In src/routes/greenCredits.js add POST /award. Accept {userId amount action referenceId description}. Create GreenCreditLedger entry. Update users.greenCredits += amount. Update users.greenTier based on new balance. Return updated user.
Notes: Called internally from returns and marketplace buy — not directly from frontend

What: Add input validation and error handling to all endpoints
How: Go through every route file. Add validation: check required fields exist check user exists before processing return check inventory item is AVAILABLE before buying. Wrap all DB calls in try/catch. Return proper HTTP status codes: 400 for bad input 404 for not found 500 for server errors with descriptive messages.
Notes: Without validation demo can crash on edge cases — judges might probe this

What: Add CORS header for Amplify frontend URL
How: In src/index.js update cors config: cors({origin: [AMPLIFY_URL 'http://localhost:5173'] credentials:true}). Replace * with specific Amplify domain once Amplify app is created. Test from deployed frontend that CORS is not blocked.
Notes: * origin works for hackathon but updating to specific domain is better practice

What: End-to-end API test with the full demo flow
How: Using the deployed EC2 backend URL run through all API calls in demo order: GET /api/users → GET /api/marketplace?user_id=user-009 → POST /api/returns → POST /api/returns/:id/method → POST /api/grading → GET /api/admin/overview → POST /api/outgrown. Verify each returns expected data.
Notes: Keep a Postman collection with all these calls saved for backup during demo

What: Performance check — add DB indexes if queries are slow
How: If any API call takes >500ms run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the slow query. The Prisma schema has @@index on common fields. If marketplace query is slow ensure index on inventory_items(status currentDcId grade) is present. Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name add_perf_indexes if needed.
Notes: Should not be needed with small seed data — but good to verify

What: Set up AWS RDS PostgreSQL instance — free tier
How: Login to AWS Console. Go to RDS. Create database: PostgreSQL 16. Template: Free Tier. Instance: db.t3.micro. Storage: 20GB gp2. DB name: reloop. Set master username and password. Enable public access temporarily for migration. Note the endpoint URL.
Notes: Region: ap-south-1 (Mumbai). VPC security group must allow port 5432 from EC2 security group

What: Update DATABASE_URL to RDS endpoint and run migrations
How: Update backend/.env with the RDS DATABASE_URL string: postgresql://user:password@rds-endpoint.amazonaws.com:5432/reloop. Run: npx prisma migrate deploy (uses existing migrations). Then run: node prisma/seed.js. Verify data in DB by querying.
Notes: migrate deploy (not dev) for production DB — does not create new migrations

What: Create AWS S3 bucket with public read access for images
How: Go to AWS S3. Create bucket: reloop-hackathon-uploads in ap-south-1. Uncheck 'Block all public access'. Add bucket policy for public read: s3:GetObject on arn:aws:s3:::reloop-hackathon-uploads/*. Update backend .env with bucket name and AWS credentials.
Notes: Public bucket is acceptable for hackathon. Real production would use CloudFront

What: Deploy Express backend to AWS EC2 t2.micro
How: Launch EC2 t2.micro (Amazon Linux 2). Set security group: allow port 22 (SSH) and 5000 (API). SSH in. Install Node 18 and PM2. Clone repo. cd backend && npm install. Create .env file with RDS URL Gemini key S3 creds. npx prisma migrate deploy && node prisma/seed.js. pm2 start src/index.js --name reloop-api. pm2 startup to survive reboots.
Notes: Test EC2 public IP: curl http://EC2-IP:5000/api/health — should return {status:ok}

What: Deploy React frontend to AWS Amplify
How: Go to AWS Amplify Console. New app: Host web app. Connect GitHub. Select repo and branch. Set build settings: baseDirectory=frontend buildCommand='npm install && npm run build' outputDirectory=dist. Add environment variable VITE_API_URL=http://EC2-IP:5000/api. Deploy.
Notes: First deploy takes 3-5 minutes. Amplify gives a free HTTPS URL — use this for demo

What: Upload product images to S3 and update image URLs in DB
How: Upload all 27 product images to S3 bucket under /images/ prefix. The images were placed in frontend/public/images/ by Dev1. Upload them to S3: aws s3 sync frontend/public/images/ s3://reloop-hackathon-uploads/images/. Update products.image_url in DB to use S3 URLs. Or configure Amplify to serve /public/images/ (simpler).
Notes: Simplest approach: keep images in frontend/public/images/ served by Amplify. No S3 needed for product images — S3 only for uploaded return photos

What: Final system health check — verify all AWS services are talking
How: Test end-to-end from Amplify URL: Browse marketplace (Amplify → EC2 → RDS). Upload a photo in return flow (Amplify → EC2 → S3 → Gemini → RDS). Verify photo URL in DB is an S3 URL. Verify marketplace shows products from RDS. Check CloudWatch for any EC2 errors.
Notes: If any AWS service fails have localhost backup ready: frontend localhost:5173 backend localhost:5000

What: Prepare demo environment — seed fresh data and verify all scenarios
How: Run node prisma/seed.js on production RDS to ensure fresh demo data. Verify in Amplify app: ret-013 (boAt earbuds) shows as RECEIVED_AT_DC with no grading. Priya's return (ret-016) shows as INITIATED triggering interception. Pop Socket shows DONATED not in marketplace. Rahul's baby monitor shows in orders page.
Notes: Create a prisma/reset-and-reseed.js script that deletes all data and re-seeds cleanly for demo reset
